calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Frazee for and with Defendant Deshawn Davis.
Cristin Dale for and with Plaintiff Ijeoma Anyanwu (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter for hearing on defendants' motion to set aside default. The Court heard testimony and received documentary evidence from both parties.

Victor Blama, a registered process server formerly employed by ABC Legal, was placed under oath and testified regarding his attempts to serve defendants. Mr. Blama stated that on May 7, 2024, he served Ms. Pittman at 2800 Ingalls Street in San Francisco, and on June 17, 2024, he effectuated substitute service on Mr. Davis at the same address. He described the procedures followed using the ABC Legal app and acknowledged that the initial proof of service for Ms. Pittman erroneously listed the address as "ABC to skip," but was later corrected in an amended proof of service.

Melissa Butler, an employee of ABC Legal, was placed under oath and testified that an internal investigation confirmed service was effectuated at 2800 Ingalls Street, and that the address in the original proof of service was prepopulated in error.

Plaintiff Ijeoma Anyanwu was placed under oath and testified that she is a former romantic partner of Mr. Davis, that 2800 Ingalls was Mr. Davis's residence during their relationship in 2023, and that she stayed there frequently. She stated there were no visible unit designations on the home and testified regarding the layout and use of entrances to the property.

Counsel presented oral argument in support of their client's positions. Ms. Dale opposed the motion, contending service was proper and timely, and that defendants failed to act with reasonable diligence. Mr. Frazzee argued the service was defective, no actual notice was received, and emphasized California's preference for resolving cases on the merits.

After hearing oral argument, the Court ruled as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is granted. Defendants shall file and serve their responsive pleading no later than July 1, 2025.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Cristin Dale, C. Dale Law, PC, 510-859-7235, via zoom for and with plaintiff, Ijeoma Anyanwu
David Frazee, Frezee Legal, 650-619-1631, in-person appearance for and with defendants, Deshawn Davis, Betty Pittman

Ms. Pittman and Mr. Davis are placed under oath and testified. Ms. Dale states that additional time is needed in order to request witnesses from ABC Legal to appear in court. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DESHAWN DAVIS , AN INDIVIDUAL, BETTY PITTMAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS & AUTHORITIES is continued to May-23-25. Parties waived notices.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Jan-03-2025 Off Calendar

Declaration Of Ijeoma Anyanwu In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:57 am. and concluding at 10:20 am.

Plaintiff Ijeoma Anyanwu is present represented by Cristin Dale, Esq., via zoom video conference, placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Deshawn Davis not appearing.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

Plaintiff confirms that the Judgment will resolve the entire matter.

COURT FINDS:
1. The DOES in this matter were dismissed on Aug-04-2022
2. Default against Defendant Carl Radford entered on
3. The 1st amended complaints were properly served on Defendants.
4. The Defendants owed a contractual duty to Plaintiff.
5. The Defendants have breached their contractual duty to Plaintiff.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Ijeoma Anyanwu, and against Defendant Deshawn Davis, as follows:

1. Damages: $32,900.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $0
3. Costs: $870.00
4. Attorney fees: $5,000.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $38,770.00

Proposed judgment signed and filed. (D505)

Declaration Of Ijeoma Anyanwu In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 585(D)
